mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 349436M as of 2004-12-01 reflects strong consumer spending momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month growth rate and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth periods produce negative SPX returns in > 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, it signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates and borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.16% as of 2004-12-01 is below restrictive threshold
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 3.0% for 2+ months, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) of more than 0.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132266K as of 2004-12-01 shows stable employment
failure_condition: When PAYEMS declines > 0.5% MoM, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal robust supply-side capacity expansion and construction activity. When both series show positive 3-month momentum simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to improved productivity and capital formation, producing positive 20-day SPX returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 117.813 and HOUST at 1771K as of 2004-12-01 both elevated
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint INDPRO+HOUST momentum signal with 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of simultaneous positive momentum in both series produce negative SPX returns in > 48% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerating above 3.0% year-over-year signals monetary policy constraints and real rate compression. In response, the Federal Reserve maintains or raises FEDFUNDS, compressing equity risk premiums and producing negative 5-day SPX returns as investors front-run policy tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 191.2 as of 2004-11-01 reflects moderate inflation
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.0%, SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between inflation acceleration and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.5% signals labor market slack and reduced consumer confidence, which depresses retail sales and corporate earnings. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% as of 2004-12-01 is near trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging labor market weakness
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.5%, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (UNRATE + RSAFS weakness) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at an annualized rate above 3.0% signals robust economic expansion and earnings growth. This always-on trend mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in sustained corporate profit growth.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 10891.0B (2017 dollars) as of 2004-07-01 reflects solid growth trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 quarterly growth rate and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or periods of GDPC1 growth > 3.0% annualized produce negative SPX returns in > 48% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month by more than 0.3% signals supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This threshold-triggered mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors anticipate earnings pressure from lower output and productivity.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 117.813 as of 2004-12-01 shows stable production levels
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging supply constraints
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 0.3% MoM, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
